title: parnassia parviflora subsp. californica
intro: Parnassia parviflora subsp. californica, also known as California grass-of-parnassus, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Celastraceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Parnassia parviflora subsp. californica include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or salves, and are often used topically to treat skin conditions and promote overall skin health.
